conversation.speaker: user
conversation.text: Setting up CI/CD for the new repo -- weighing GitHub Actions, CircleCI, and Jenkins.

conversation.speaker: user
conversation.text: Jenkins gives us the most control but somebody has to host and maintain the server ourselves.

conversation.speaker: user
conversation.text: CircleCI has a solid reputation but it's yet another vendor and dashboard to manage.

conversation.speaker: user
conversation.text: I need to pick up a prescription refill from the pharmacy.

conversation.speaker: user
conversation.text: GitHub Actions is already tied into the repo, so we wouldn't need to run a separate service for it.

conversation.speaker: user
conversation.text: Picked up the prescription, pharmacy line was surprisingly short today.

conversation.speaker: user
conversation.text: Set up a quick GitHub Actions workflow and it was running in under twenty minutes.

conversation.speaker: user
conversation.text: Jenkins would take at least a day just to get the server provisioned and configured.

conversation.speaker: user
conversation.text: My upstairs neighbor is having some work done, has been noisy all week.

conversation.speaker: user
conversation.text: Confirmed GitHub Actions supports everything we currently need without any extra plugins.

conversation.speaker: user
conversation.text: Going with GitHub Actions. It's already tied into the repo, there's nothing extra to host, and it does everything we need out of the box.

query: Which CI/CD provider did the team decide to use?
